danger_signals.meaning: Immediate lethal threat via direct contact
Dual threat - lethal on contact AND ranged fire-breathing attack
Ranged attack requiring spatial awareness and evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Multiple Pookas/Fygars moving toward Taizo Hori
danger_signals.meaning: Increasing danger as enemy coordination escalates

safety_signals.visual_form: Spatial separation on overhead island map
safety_signals.meaning: Player safety correlates with physical distance from threat entities
Natural barrier that kills all creatures - acts as area containment and escape mechanism

safety_signals.visual_form: Ground fractures appearing on map via jackhammer
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency tool for environmental control and threat elimination

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Active presence with air pump and jackhammer tools - visible on overhead island map
Scattered across island terrain, mobile threat entities
Island-distributed enemies with enhanced attack range via fire projection

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Round active - enemies present on island
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pookas and Fygars visible on overhead map with intact terrain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol/chase player; player must navigate and execute tactics

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Land mass reduced via fault system
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Portions of island sink into ocean; progressively smaller playable area
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Compressed terrain forces closer player-enemy interactions; enemies remain trapped on shrinking islands

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Bonus state - three land masses separated
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Vegetable item appears on remaining island
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player incentivized to collect bonus while managing threats

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Round ending condition - one to three enemies remain
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Surviving enemies move toward island edge
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies retreat to water and self-eliminate; round automatically concludes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple Pookas and Fygars densely distributed on map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Coordinated enemy movement; increased fire-breathing frequency; player must use environmental hazards (fault lines) strategically

environmental_storytelling.example: Overhead island map visually establishes bounded play space; water perimeter serves as both world-state boundary and mechanical hazard, reinforcing isolation theme
Fault line system creates visual record of player progress - each separated land mass represents completed objective, world visibly transforms with each round

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy behavior change as world state shifts
environmental_storytelling.example: When land masses shrink, enemy behavior escalates from patrol to aggressive pursuit, and final enemies flee to water - visual-behavioral feedback loop communicates mounting pressure then collapse
Vegetable appearance on fragmented terrain signals reward opportunity and reinforces spatial mastery - placement visually acknowledges player's successful island reduction strategy

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves across island terrain seeking player contact
Pursues player with intent to make lethal contact
Moves across island terrain; intermittently breathes fire in player direction
Pursues player while executing ranged fire attacks; dual threat modality
Coordinate movement toward island edge; self-eliminate by jumping into water when few remain
Navigates island, inflates enemies or creates fault lines to eliminate threats and separate terrain

notes: Dig Dug II employs a minimalist faction system with clear enemy/player distinction. The core world-state communication occurs through environmental transformation - the shrinking island acts as visual feedback for player progress and increasing pressure. Enemy color-coding (red Pookas vs. dragon-form Fygars) provides immediate threat differentiation. The overhead perspective is critical to manifestation design: it enables simultaneous display of all threats, environmental hazards, and player agency tools. Fire-breathing is the primary visual differentiator for Fygar danger escalation beyond Pooka contact-threat. Water boundary functions as both cosmetic world-edge and active mechanical hazard, reinforcing spatial constraint narrative. Bonus vegetable appearance serves dual function: reward signal and world-state indicator (only appears after successful terrain separation). No faction-based political narrative exists; conflict is purely mechanical/survival-based.
